title: Asclepius: The Perfect Discourse
source: Augustine, De Civitate Dei; Latin manuscripts
description: Hermes prophesies the decline of Egyptian religion and the death of the gods — a passage that haunted Augustine.
introduction: Hermes prophesies the decline of Egyptian religion and the death of the gods — a passage that haunted Augustine.

translation.text: O Egypt, Egypt, of your religion nothing will remain but an empty tale, which your own children will not believe.
